summary: Dependency-confusion exfiltration package beacons host/network/process data to Burp Collaborator on install
details: ttspc-server-sample@99.9.2 is a dependency-confusion package that auto-executes a reconnaissance and exfiltration payload at install time. package.json declares `"postinstall": "node index.js"`, and index.js collects the installer's hostname, username, working directory, platform, public IP (via api.ipify.org), WHOIS data (rdap.arin.net), internal interface IPs, the full running process list (`ps aux --sort=-%mem` on Unix / `tasklist /V /FO CSV` on Windows via execSync), pid/argv/execPath, and the presence of environment variables `APP_KEY`, `APP_SECRET`, `APP_SERVICE_ID`, `TIKTOK_SHOP_ID`, and `TEST_USER_PASSWORD`. The collected JSON is POSTed over plain HTTP to a hardcoded Burp Collaborator endpoint at 806lcq31v2jj4qqt9555xy6x1o7pvqjf.oastify.com (index.js line 15). The package is published at version 99.9.2 to win npm version resolution against an internal package of the same name, and ships no actual implementation of the advertised TikTok Shop server functionality. Any `npm install` of this package leaks installer host identity, network topology, process state, and credential-env-var presence to an attacker-controlled callback host.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
